The Ospedale degli Innocenti, one of the most famous buildings in  ...    fifteenth-century Italy. This foundling hospital is named in memory of “the Innocents,” the children of Bethlehem massacred by King Herod when he had been tricked by the wise men (Matthew 2:16-18). It is on the  ...    The Innocenti opened in 1445 and admitted sixty-two infants.1 Unlike other Florentine hospitals that accepted some children along with the sick and poor, the Innocenti was totally devoted to newborns and foundlings from the countryside as well as the city.
